known_good.note: Classic peaceful community pairing when both are kept in proper groups.
Generally workable, with normal planted community planning.
Small peaceful nano species using different parts of the tank.
Common peaceful community pairing with compatible adult size and behavior.
Active cooler-water schooling fish; usually workable with enough swimming room.
Peaceful small community fish using different tank zones.

known_caution.note: Caution due to betta temperament and activity/social mismatch, not predation.
Often attempted, but fin-nipping/activity/territory context matters.
Fin-nipping risk should cap at caution rather than being treated as predation.
Only a narrow, heavily planted, carefully monitored setup.
Possible water and tank-size overlap, but not a simple community recommendation.
Fin-nipping risk with slow, long-finned angelfish.

known_bad.note: Pea puffer fin-nipping and species-only tendency make this strongly unsuitable.
Mouth-gape predation risk, especially with small slender tetras.
Surface predator with small slender fish.
Large aggressive cichlid with small slender prey fish.

controversial_context_dependent.note: One of the more defensible pea puffer tankmates, but still context dependent.
Territory and footprint conflict; not automatic predation.
Temperature/activity/fin vulnerability can make this poor depending on setup.
Hard-water overlap, but mbuna behavior makes it context dependent.
